etymology.description: 言 ("words, to speak") + 八 ("breath coming from the mouth; divide") + 厃 ("danger") = speak too much
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 言(言) (yán, "words, to speak"), 八(八) (bā, "breath coming from the mouth; divide"), and 厃 to suggest speaking excessively, perhaps to the point of causing trouble or reaching a dangerous limit.

parts.english: breath coming from the mouth; divide

examples.chinese: 她用围裙兜着水果(她用圍裙兜著水果)，詹着几个苹果(詹著幾個蘋果)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng wéiqún dōu zhe shuǐguǒ, zhān zhe jǐ gè píngguǒ.
examples.english: She used her apron to hold the fruits, carrying a few apples by hand.

examples.chinese: 他小心地詹着水壶(他小心地詹著水壺)，怕洒出来(怕灑出來)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiǎoxīn de zhān zhe shuǐhú, pà sǎ chūlái.
examples.english: He carefully carried the kettle by hand, afraid it would spill.

examples.chinese: 老人用手帕詹着糖果(老人用手帕詹著糖果)，送给孩子们(送給孩子們)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎorén yòng shǒupà zhān zhe tángguǒ, sòng gěi háizimen.
examples.english: The old man carried the candies by hand using a handkerchief and gave them to the children.
